name: Can a handyman help get my house ready to sell or rent?
headline: Can a handyman help get my house ready to sell or rent?

mainEntity.name: Can a handyman help get my house ready to sell or rent?
mainEntity.text: Can a handyman help get my house ready to sell or rent?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, many handymen are ideal for **pre‑sale or pre‑rental fixes**, because they can handle a wide range of small repairs in one visit. Typical tasks before selling or renting include: - Patching nail holes and minor drywall damage - Recaulking tubs, showers, and sinks so they look clean and sealed - Fixing doors that don’t latch, sticky windows, or broken locks - Replacing outdated or broken light fixtures and switches - Repairing small fence or deck problems that show up in inspections Benefits of using a handyman: - One person can handle many punch‑list items - Faster turnaround compared to scheduling several different trades - Often more affordable for cosmetic and minor functional issues Pitfalls to avoid: - Only treating the “visible” problem without checking for deeper issues, like hidden leaks or serious electrical problems - Waiting until right before listing or move‑in day. Schedules might be full, and rushed work can lead to mistakes A good strategy is to walk through the property with a notebook, write down everything that looks worn, broken, or unsafe, and then ask a handyman which items they can take on. For anything they cannot or should not handle, you can then call a specialist.
